contraventionDescription: Parked displaying multiple pay and display tickets where prohibited – Contravention Code 09

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.
2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.
3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.

2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.

3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
1. This contravention is similar to “meter feeding” on a bay and display bay. In some areas you are not allowed to feed the meter or to purchase more than one pay and display ticket. You should insert the full amount of money for the time required into the pay and display machine at the time when you park. So if you purchased a pay and display ticket for say 15 minutes and then after 10 minutes you purchased another for an hour you will have committed a contravention because you are not allowed to purchase more than one ticket whilst in the same pay and display bay.
2. Does it clearly say in the terms and conditions on the machine or on the road side signage that you are not allowed to purchase multiple tickets or that you must insert the full amount for the period required at the time of parking? Obviously if this isn’t clear you should appeal.
3. If you inadvertently left a previous pay and display ticket in your windscreen from a previous event you should not be penalised and you should write in.
 
4. Remember that you may cite compassionate grounds in any appeal or representation.
Please see our section on parking adjudicators decisions which will have details of cases for this contravention.
Back to Parking
